authors_text: Lei Zhang,Yu Pan,Yi Liu,Qibin Zheng,Zhisong Pan
title: KGRGRL: A User's Permission Reasoning Method Based on Knowledge Graph Reward Guidance Reinforcement Learning
title_zh: KGRGRL：一种基于知识图奖励引导强化学习的用户权限推理方法
abstract: In general, multiple domain cyberspace security assessments can be implemented by reasoning user's permissions. However, while existing methods include some information from the physical and social domains, they do not provide a comprehensive representation of cyberspace. Existing reasoning methods are also based on expert-given rules, resulting in inefficiency and a low degree of intelligence. To address this challenge, we create a Knowledge Graph (KG) of multiple domain cyberspace in order to provide a standard semantic description of the multiple domain cyberspace. Following that, we proposed a user's permissions reasoning method based on reinforcement learning. All permissions in cyberspace are represented as nodes, and an agent is trained to find all permissions that user can have according to user's initial permissions and cyberspace KG. We set 10 reward setting rules based on the features of cyberspace KG in the reinforcement learning of reward information setting, so that the agent can better locate user's all permissions and avoid blindly finding user's permissions. The results of the experiments showed that the proposed method can successfully reason about user's permissions and increase the intelligence level of the user's permissions reasoning method. At the same time, the F1 value of the proposed method is 6% greater than that of the Translating Embedding (TransE) method.
summary: In general, multiple domain cyberspace security assessments can be implemented by reasoning user's permissions. However, while existing methods include some information from the physical and social domains, they do not provide a comprehensive representation of cyberspace. Existing reasoning methods are also based on expert-given rules, resulting in inefficiency and a low degree of intelligence. To address this challenge, we create a Knowledge Graph (KG) of multiple domain cyberspace in order to provide a standard semantic description of the multiple domain cyberspace. Following that, we proposed a user's permissions reasoning method based on reinforcement learning. All permissions in cyberspace are represented as nodes, and an agent is trained to find all permissions that user can have according to user's initial permissions and cyberspace KG. We set 10 reward setting rules based on the features of cyberspace KG in the reinforcement learning of reward information setting, so that the agent can better locate user's all permissions and avoid blindly finding user's permissions. The results of the experiments showed that the proposed method can successfully reason about user's permissions and increase the intelligence level of the user's permissions reasoning method. At the same time, the F1 value of the proposed method is 6% greater than that of the Translating Embedding (TransE) method.
abstract_zh: 一般来说，多域网络空间安全评估可以通过推理用户权限来实现。然而，虽然现有方法包括来自物理和社会领域的一些信息，但它们并没有提供网络空间的全面表示。现有的推理方法也是基于专家给出的规则，导致效率低、智能程度低。为了应对这一挑战，我们创建了多域网络空间的知识图（KG），以提供多域网络空间的标准语义描述。随后，我们提出了一种基于强化学习的用户权限推理方法。将网络空间中的所有权限表示为节点，训练一个代理根据用户的初始权限和网络空间KG找到用户可以拥有的所有权限。在奖励信息设置的强化学习中，我们根据网络空间知识图谱的特点，设置了10条奖励设置规则，使代理能够更好地定位用户的所有权限，避免盲目寻找用户的权限。实验结果表明，该方法能够成功推理用户权限，提高了用户权限推理方法的智能水平。同时，该方法的F1值比平移嵌入（TransE）方法大6%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于知识图谱和强化学习的用户权限推理方法KGRGRL，用于多域网络空间安全评估。
AI.motivation: 现有方法依赖专家规则且无法全面表征网络空间，导致权限推理效率低、智能化程度不足。
AI.method: 构建多域网络空间知识图谱，将权限表示为节点，通过强化学习训练智能体，并设置10条奖励规则引导权限发现过程。
AI.result: 实验表明该方法F1值比TransE方法提升6%，能有效推理用户权限。
AI.conclusion: KGRGRL方法提升了权限推理的智能化水平，为多域网络空间安全评估提供了新思路。

search_text: KGRGRL: A User's Permission Reasoning Method Based on Knowledge Graph Reward Guidance Reinforcement Learning KGRGRL：一种基于知识图奖励引导强化学习的用户权限推理方法 In general, multiple domain cyberspace security assessments can be implemented by reasoning user's permissions. However, while existing methods include some information from the physical and social domains, they do not provide a comprehensive representation of cyberspace. Existing reasoning methods are also based on expert-given rules, resulting in inefficiency and a low degree of intelligence. To address this challenge, we create a Knowledge Graph (KG) of multiple domain cyberspace in order to provide a standard semantic description of the multiple domain cyberspace. Following that, we proposed a user's permissions reasoning method based on reinforcement learning. All permissions in cyberspace are represented as nodes, and an agent is trained to find all permissions that user can have according to user's initial permissions and cyberspace KG. We set 10 reward setting rules based on the features of cyberspace KG in the reinforcement learning of reward information setting, so that the agent can better locate user's all permissions and avoid blindly finding user's permissions. The results of the experiments showed that the proposed method can successfully reason about user's permissions and increase the intelligence level of the user's permissions reasoning method. At the same time, the F1 value of the proposed method is 6% greater than that of the Translating Embedding (TransE) method. 一般来说，多域网络空间安全评估可以通过推理用户权限来实现。然而，虽然现有方法包括来自物理和社会领域的一些信息，但它们并没有提供网络空间的全面表示。现有的推理方法也是基于专家给出的规则，导致效率低、智能程度低。为了应对这一挑战，我们创建了多域网络空间的知识图（KG），以提供多域网络空间的标准语义描述。随后，我们提出了一种基于强化学习的用户权限推理方法。将网络空间中的所有权限表示为节点，训练一个代理根据用户的初始权限和网络空间KG找到用户可以拥有的所有权限。在奖励信息设置的强化学习中，我们根据网络空间知识图谱的特点，设置了10条奖励设置规则，使代理能够更好地定位用户的所有权限，避免盲目寻找用户的权限。实验结果表明，该方法能够成功推理用户权限，提高了用户权限推理方法的智能水平。同时，该方法的F1值比平移嵌入（TransE）方法大6%。 本文提出一种基于知识图谱和强化学习的用户权限推理方法KGRGRL，用于多域网络空间安全评估。 Lei Zhang,Yu Pan,Yi Liu,Qibin Zheng,Zhisong Pan cs.AI,cs.CR,cs.LG

title: Computing Bend-Minimum Orthogonal Drawings of Plane Series-Parallel Graphs in Linear Time
title_zh: 线性时间内计算平面串并联图的最小弯曲正交图
comment: arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2008.03784
comments: arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2008.03784
abstract: A planar orthogonal drawing of a planar 4-graph G (i.e., a planar graph with vertex-degree at most four) is a crossing-free drawing that maps each vertex of G to a distinct point of the plane and each edge of $G$ to a sequence of horizontal and vertical segments between its end-points. A longstanding open question in Graph Drawing, dating back over 30 years, is whether there exists a linear-time algorithm to compute an orthogonal drawing of a plane 4-graph with the minimum number of bends. The term "plane" indicates that the input graph comes together with a planar embedding, which must be preserved by the drawing (i.e., the drawing must have the same set of faces as the input graph). In this paper, we positively answer the question above for the widely-studied class of series-parallel graphs. Our linear-time algorithm is based on a characterization of the planar series-parallel graphs that admit an orthogonal drawing without bends. This characterization is given in terms of the orthogonal spirality that each type of triconnected component of the graph can take; the orthogonal spirality of a component measures how much that component is "rolled-up" in an orthogonal drawing of the graph.
summary: A planar orthogonal drawing of a planar 4-graph G (i.e., a planar graph with vertex-degree at most four) is a crossing-free drawing that maps each vertex of G to a distinct point of the plane and each edge of $G$ to a sequence of horizontal and vertical segments between its end-points. A longstanding open question in Graph Drawing, dating back over 30 years, is whether there exists a linear-time algorithm to compute an orthogonal drawing of a plane 4-graph with the minimum number of bends. The term "plane" indicates that the input graph comes together with a planar embedding, which must be preserved by the drawing (i.e., the drawing must have the same set of faces as the input graph). In this paper, we positively answer the question above for the widely-studied class of series-parallel graphs. Our linear-time algorithm is based on a characterization of the planar series-parallel graphs that admit an orthogonal drawing without bends. This characterization is given in terms of the orthogonal spirality that each type of triconnected component of the graph can take; the orthogonal spirality of a component measures how much that component is "rolled-up" in an orthogonal drawing of the graph.
abstract_zh: 平面 4-图 G（即顶点度数最多为 4 的平面图）的平面正交图是一种无交叉图，它将 G 的每个顶点映射到平面的不同点，并将 $G$ 的每条边映射到其端点之间的一系列水平和垂直线段。图形绘制中一个长期悬而未决的问题可以追溯到 30 多年前，即是否存在线性时间算法来计算具有最少弯曲次数的平面 4 图的正交绘图。术语“平面”表示输入图与平面嵌入结合在一起，该嵌入必须由绘图保留（即绘图必须具有与输入图相同的面集）。在本文中，我们针对广泛研究的串并联图类积极回答了上述问题。我们的线性时间算法基于平面串并联图的表征，该图允许无弯曲的正交绘图。该特征是根据图形的每种类型的三元连通分量可以采用的正交螺旋性给出的；组件的正交螺旋度衡量该组件在图形的正交绘图中“卷起”的程度。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种在线性时间内计算平面系列并行图的最小弯折正交绘制的算法。
AI.motivation: 正交绘制的最小弯折问题在图绘制领域长期未解，尤其针对平面4图的线性时间算法需求迫切。
AI.method: 通过分析平面系列并行图的三连通组件正交螺旋度特征，构建线性时间算法框架。
AI.result: 成功证明系列并行图可在线性时间内生成无交叉且弯折最少的正交绘制。
AI.conclusion: 该成果解决了30年来的理论难题，为相关图绘制应用提供了高效算法基础。

title: Efficient Knowledge Compilation Beyond Weighted Model Counting
title_zh: 超越加权模型计数的高效知识编译
comment: Paper presented at the 38th International Conference on Logic Programming (ICLP 2022), 16 pages
comments: Paper presented at the 38th International Conference on Logic Programming (ICLP 2022), 16 pages
abstract: Quantitative extensions of logic programming often require the solution of so called second level inference tasks, i.e., problems that involve a third operation, such as maximization or normalization, on top of addition and multiplication, and thus go beyond the well-known weighted or algebraic model counting setting of probabilistic logic programming under the distribution semantics. We introduce Second Level Algebraic Model Counting (2AMC) as a generic framework for these kinds of problems. As 2AMC is to (algebraic) model counting what forall-exists-SAT is to propositional satisfiability, it is notoriously hard to solve. First level techniques based on Knowledge Compilation (KC) have been adapted for specific 2AMC instances by imposing variable order constraints on the resulting circuit. However, those constraints can severely increase the circuit size and thus decrease the efficiency of such approaches. We show that we can exploit the logical structure of a 2AMC problem to omit parts of these constraints, thus limiting the negative effect. Furthermore, we introduce and implement a strategy to generate a sufficient set of constraints statically, with a priori guarantees for the performance of KC. Our empirical evaluation on several benchmarks and tasks confirms that our theoretical results can translate into more efficient solving in practice. Under consideration for acceptance in TPLP.
summary: Quantitative extensions of logic programming often require the solution of so called second level inference tasks, i.e., problems that involve a third operation, such as maximization or normalization, on top of addition and multiplication, and thus go beyond the well-known weighted or algebraic model counting setting of probabilistic logic programming under the distribution semantics. We introduce Second Level Algebraic Model Counting (2AMC) as a generic framework for these kinds of problems. As 2AMC is to (algebraic) model counting what forall-exists-SAT is to propositional satisfiability, it is notoriously hard to solve. First level techniques based on Knowledge Compilation (KC) have been adapted for specific 2AMC instances by imposing variable order constraints on the resulting circuit. However, those constraints can severely increase the circuit size and thus decrease the efficiency of such approaches. We show that we can exploit the logical structure of a 2AMC problem to omit parts of these constraints, thus limiting the negative effect. Furthermore, we introduce and implement a strategy to generate a sufficient set of constraints statically, with a priori guarantees for the performance of KC. Our empirical evaluation on several benchmarks and tasks confirms that our theoretical results can translate into more efficient solving in practice. Under consideration for acceptance in TPLP.
abstract_zh: 逻辑编程的定量扩展通常需要解决所谓的二级推理任务，即在加法和乘法之上涉及第三种运算的问题，例如最大化或归一化，从而超出分布语义下概率逻辑编程的众所周知的加权或代数模型计数设置。我们引入二级代数模型计数（2AMC）作为此类问题的通用框架。由于 2AMC 是计算（代数）模型，就像 forall-exists-SAT 是命题可满足性一样，众所周知，它很难解决。基于知识编译 (KC) 的第一级技术已通过对结果电路施加可变顺序约束来适应特定的 2AMC 实例。然而，这些限制会严重增加电路尺寸，从而降低此类方法的效率。我们证明，我们可以利用 2AMC 问题的逻辑结构来忽略部分约束，从而限制负面影响。此外，我们引入并实现了一种策略来静态生成一组足够的约束，并为 KC 的性能提供先验保证。我们对几个基准和任务的实证评估证实，我们的理论结果可以转化为实践中更有效的解决方案。正在考虑接受 TPLP。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为第二级代数模型计数（2AMC）的框架，用于解决超出加权模型计数的逻辑编程问题，并通过优化知识编译方法提高求解效率。
AI.motivation: 现有的知识编译技术在处理需要额外操作（如最大化或归一化）的第二级推理任务时，因变量顺序约束导致电路规模膨胀，效率低下。
AI.method: 通过利用2AMC问题的逻辑结构，省略部分约束条件，并设计静态生成约束集的策略，以保证知识编译的性能。
AI.result: 实验表明，该方法在多个基准任务中显著提升了求解效率，验证了理论分析的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 本文为解决第二级推理任务提供了更高效的知识编译方法，为概率逻辑编程的扩展应用奠定了基础。

search_text: Efficient Knowledge Compilation Beyond Weighted Model Counting 超越加权模型计数的高效知识编译 Quantitative extensions of logic programming often require the solution of so called second level inference tasks, i.e., problems that involve a third operation, such as maximization or normalization, on top of addition and multiplication, and thus go beyond the well-known weighted or algebraic model counting setting of probabilistic logic programming under the distribution semantics. We introduce Second Level Algebraic Model Counting (2AMC) as a generic framework for these kinds of problems. As 2AMC is to (algebraic) model counting what forall-exists-SAT is to propositional satisfiability, it is notoriously hard to solve. First level techniques based on Knowledge Compilation (KC) have been adapted for specific 2AMC instances by imposing variable order constraints on the resulting circuit. However, those constraints can severely increase the circuit size and thus decrease the efficiency of such approaches. We show that we can exploit the logical structure of a 2AMC problem to omit parts of these constraints, thus limiting the negative effect. Furthermore, we introduce and implement a strategy to generate a sufficient set of constraints statically, with a priori guarantees for the performance of KC. Our empirical evaluation on several benchmarks and tasks confirms that our theoretical results can translate into more efficient solving in practice. Under consideration for acceptance in TPLP. 逻辑编程的定量扩展通常需要解决所谓的二级推理任务，即在加法和乘法之上涉及第三种运算的问题，例如最大化或归一化，从而超出分布语义下概率逻辑编程的众所周知的加权或代数模型计数设置。我们引入二级代数模型计数（2AMC）作为此类问题的通用框架。由于 2AMC 是计算（代数）模型，就像 forall-exists-SAT 是命题可满足性一样，众所周知，它很难解决。基于知识编译 (KC) 的第一级技术已通过对结果电路施加可变顺序约束来适应特定的 2AMC 实例。然而，这些限制会严重增加电路尺寸，从而降低此类方法的效率。我们证明，我们可以利用 2AMC 问题的逻辑结构来忽略部分约束，从而限制负面影响。此外，我们引入并实现了一种策略来静态生成一组足够的约束，并为 KC 的性能提供先验保证。我们对几个基准和任务的实证评估证实，我们的理论结果可以转化为实践中更有效的解决方案。正在考虑接受 TPLP。 本文提出了一种名为第二级代数模型计数（2AMC）的框架，用于解决超出加权模型计数的逻辑编程问题，并通过优化知识编译方法提高求解效率。 Rafael Kiesel,Pietro Totis,Angelika Kimmig cs.AI,cs.LO

authors_text: Unnikrishnan R Nair,Sarthak Sharma,Udit Singh Parihar,Midhun S Menon,Srikanth Vidapanakal
title: Bridging Sim2Real Gap Using Image Gradients for the Task of End-to-End Autonomous Driving
title_zh: 使用图像梯度弥合 Sim2Real 差距以完成端到端自动驾驶任务
abstract: We present the first prize solution to NeurIPS 2021 - AWS Deepracer Challenge. In this competition, the task was to train a reinforcement learning agent (i.e. an autonomous car), that learns to drive by interacting with its environment, a simulated track, by taking an action in a given state to maximize the expected reward. This model was then tested on a real-world track with a miniature AWS Deepracer car. Our goal is to train a model that can complete a lap as fast as possible without going off the track. The Deepracer challenge is a part of a series of embodied intelligence competitions in the field of autonomous vehicles, called The AI Driving Olympics (AI-DO). The overall objective of the AI-DO is to provide accessible mechanisms for benchmarking progress in autonomy applied to the task of autonomous driving. The tricky section of this challenge was the sim2real transfer of the learned skills. To reduce the domain gap in the observation space we did a canny edge detection in addition to cropping out of the unnecessary background information. We modeled the problem as a behavioral cloning task and used MLP-MIXER to optimize for runtime. We made sure our model was capable of handling control noise by careful filtration of the training data and that gave us a robust model capable of completing the track even when 50% of the commands were randomly changed. The overall runtime of the model was only 2-3ms on a modern CPU.
summary: We present the first prize solution to NeurIPS 2021 - AWS Deepracer Challenge. In this competition, the task was to train a reinforcement learning agent (i.e. an autonomous car), that learns to drive by interacting with its environment, a simulated track, by taking an action in a given state to maximize the expected reward. This model was then tested on a real-world track with a miniature AWS Deepracer car. Our goal is to train a model that can complete a lap as fast as possible without going off the track. The Deepracer challenge is a part of a series of embodied intelligence competitions in the field of autonomous vehicles, called The AI Driving Olympics (AI-DO). The overall objective of the AI-DO is to provide accessible mechanisms for benchmarking progress in autonomy applied to the task of autonomous driving. The tricky section of this challenge was the sim2real transfer of the learned skills. To reduce the domain gap in the observation space we did a canny edge detection in addition to cropping out of the unnecessary background information. We modeled the problem as a behavioral cloning task and used MLP-MIXER to optimize for runtime. We made sure our model was capable of handling control noise by careful filtration of the training data and that gave us a robust model capable of completing the track even when 50% of the commands were randomly changed. The overall runtime of the model was only 2-3ms on a modern CPU.
abstract_zh: 我们向 NeurIPS 2021 颁发一等奖解决方案 - AWS Deepracer 挑战赛。在本次比赛中，任务是训练强化学习代理（即自动驾驶汽车），该代理通过与环境（模拟赛道）交互来学习驾驶，在给定状态下采取行动以最大化预期奖励。然后使用微型 AWS Deepracer 赛车在真实赛道上测试该模型。我们的目标是训练一个能够在不脱离赛道的情况下尽可能快地完成一圈的模型。 Deepracer 挑战赛是自动驾驶汽车领域一系列名为人工智能驾驶奥运会 (AI-DO) 的具体智能竞赛的一部分。 AI-DO 的总体目标是提供可访问的机制，用于对应用于自动驾驶任务的自主进展进行基准测试。这项挑战的棘手部分是所学技能的模拟到真实迁移。为了减少观察空间中的域间隙，除了裁剪掉不必要的背景信息之外，我们还进行了精明的边缘检测。我们将该问题建模为行为克隆任务，并使用 MLP-MIXER 来优化运行时。我们通过仔细过滤训练数据来确保我们的模型能够处理控制噪声，这为我们提供了一个强大的模型，即使在 50% 的命令随机更改的情况下也能够完成赛道。该模型的整体运行时间在现代 CPU 上仅为 2-3 毫秒。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在通过图像梯度解决模拟环境到现实环境的迁移问题，实现端到端自动驾驶。
AI.motivation: 解决模拟环境与真实环境间的领域差异，提升自动驾驶模型在实际场景中的泛化能力。
AI.method: 采用Canny边缘检测与背景裁剪减少领域差异，基于行为克隆任务使用MLP-MIXER模型优化运行效率，并通过训练数据过滤增强模型对控制噪声的鲁棒性。
AI.result: 模型在真实赛道上成功完成比赛，即使50%控制指令被随机干扰仍能稳定运行，模型在现代CPU上的推理时间仅为2-3毫秒。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效弥合了模拟与现实间的差距，证明了基于图像梯度处理与行为克隆的端到端自动驾驶方案在真实环境中的高效性与鲁棒性。

title: Topologically Persistent Features-based Object Recognition in Cluttered Indoor Environments
title_zh: 杂乱室内环境中基于拓扑持久特征的目标识别
comment: Accepted for presentation in the IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA) 2022 Workshop on Robotic Perception and Mapping: Emerging Techniques
comments: Accepted for presentation in the IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA) 2022 Workshop on Robotic Perception and Mapping: Emerging Techniques
abstract: Recognition of occluded objects in unseen indoor environments is a challenging problem for mobile robots. This work proposes a new slicing-based topological descriptor that captures the 3D shape of object point clouds to address this challenge. It yields similarities between the descriptors of the occluded and the corresponding unoccluded objects, enabling object unity-based recognition using a library of trained models. The descriptor is obtained by partitioning an object's point cloud into multiple 2D slices and constructing filtrations (nested sequences of simplicial complexes) on the slices to mimic further slicing of the slices, thereby capturing detailed shapes through persistent homology-generated features. We use nine different sequences of cluttered scenes from a benchmark dataset for performance evaluation. Our method outperforms two state-of-the-art deep learning-based point cloud classification methods, namely, DGCNN and SimpleView.
summary: Recognition of occluded objects in unseen indoor environments is a challenging problem for mobile robots. This work proposes a new slicing-based topological descriptor that captures the 3D shape of object point clouds to address this challenge. It yields similarities between the descriptors of the occluded and the corresponding unoccluded objects, enabling object unity-based recognition using a library of trained models. The descriptor is obtained by partitioning an object's point cloud into multiple 2D slices and constructing filtrations (nested sequences of simplicial complexes) on the slices to mimic further slicing of the slices, thereby capturing detailed shapes through persistent homology-generated features. We use nine different sequences of cluttered scenes from a benchmark dataset for performance evaluation. Our method outperforms two state-of-the-art deep learning-based point cloud classification methods, namely, DGCNN and SimpleView.
abstract_zh: 对于移动机器人来说，在看不见的室内环境中识别被遮挡的物体是一个具有挑战性的问题。这项工作提出了一种新的基于切片的拓扑描述符，可以捕获对象点云的 3D 形状来应对这一挑战。它产生被遮挡物体和相应的未被遮挡物体的描述符之间的相似性，从而使用经过训练的模型库实现基于物体统一的识别。该描述符是通过将对象的点云划分为多个 2D 切片并在切片上构建过滤（单纯复合体的嵌套序列）以模拟切片的进一步切片来获得的，从而通过持久同源生成的特征捕获详细形状。我们使用基准数据集中的九个不同的杂乱场景序列进行性能评估。我们的方法优于两种最先进的基于深度学习的点云分类方法，即 DGCNN 和 SimpleView。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于拓扑持久特征的三维点云物体识别方法，用于解决复杂室内环境中遮挡物体的识别问题。
AI.motivation: 移动机器人在未见过的室内环境中识别被遮挡物体具有挑战性，现有深度学习方法在复杂场景下表现不足。
AI.method: 通过将物体点云分割为二维切片，构建拓扑过滤序列并利用持久同调生成特征描述符，实现遮挡物体与完整物体的特征相似性匹配。
AI.result: 在九组杂乱场景数据集测试中，该方法优于DGCNN和SimpleView两种先进深度学习点云分类方法。
AI.conclusion: 基于拓扑持久特征的描述符能有效捕捉物体形状细节，为复杂室内环境中的遮挡物体识别提供了新思路。

authors_text: Ruan van der Merwe,Gregory Newman,Etienne Barnard
title: Manifold Characteristics That Predict Downstream Task Performance
title_zh: 预测下游任务绩效的流形特征
abstract: Pretraining methods are typically compared by evaluating the accuracy of linear classifiers, transfer learning performance, or visually inspecting the representation manifold's (RM) lower-dimensional projections. We show that the differences between methods can be understood more clearly by investigating the RM directly, which allows for a more detailed comparison. To this end, we propose a framework and new metric to measure and compare different RMs. We also investigate and report on the RM characteristics for various pretraining methods. These characteristics are measured by applying sequentially larger local alterations to the input data, using white noise injections and Projected Gradient Descent (PGD) adversarial attacks, and then tracking each datapoint. We calculate the total distance moved for each datapoint and the relative change in distance between successive alterations. We show that self-supervised methods learn an RM where alterations lead to large but constant size changes, indicating a smoother RM than fully supervised methods. We then combine these measurements into one metric, the Representation Manifold Quality Metric (RMQM), where larger values indicate larger and less variable step sizes, and show that RMQM correlates positively with performance on downstream tasks.
summary: Pretraining methods are typically compared by evaluating the accuracy of linear classifiers, transfer learning performance, or visually inspecting the representation manifold's (RM) lower-dimensional projections. We show that the differences between methods can be understood more clearly by investigating the RM directly, which allows for a more detailed comparison. To this end, we propose a framework and new metric to measure and compare different RMs. We also investigate and report on the RM characteristics for various pretraining methods. These characteristics are measured by applying sequentially larger local alterations to the input data, using white noise injections and Projected Gradient Descent (PGD) adversarial attacks, and then tracking each datapoint. We calculate the total distance moved for each datapoint and the relative change in distance between successive alterations. We show that self-supervised methods learn an RM where alterations lead to large but constant size changes, indicating a smoother RM than fully supervised methods. We then combine these measurements into one metric, the Representation Manifold Quality Metric (RMQM), where larger values indicate larger and less variable step sizes, and show that RMQM correlates positively with performance on downstream tasks.
abstract_zh: 通常通过评估线性分类器的准确性、迁移学习性能或目视检查表示流形 (RM) 的低维投影来比较预训练方法。我们表明，通过直接研究 RM 可以更清楚地理解方法之间的差异，从而可以进行更详细的比较。为此，我们提出了一个框架和新指标来衡量和比较不同的 RM。我们还调查并报告了各种预训练方法的 RM 特征。这些特征是通过对输入数据连续进行较大的局部更改、使用白噪声注入和投影梯度下降 (PGD) 对抗性攻击，然后跟踪每个数据点来测量的。我们计算每个数据点移动的总距离以及连续更改之间距离的相对变化。我们表明，自监督方法学习 RM，其中更改会导致较大但恒定的大小变化，这表明 RM 比完全监督方法更平滑。然后，我们将这些测量结果组合成一个指标，即表示流形质量指标 (RMQM)，其中较大的值表示较大且变化较小的步长，并表明 RMQM 与下游任务的性能呈正相关。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种新的度量方法（RMQM），用于直接分析预训练方法的表示流形（RM）特性，并将其与下游任务性能相关联。
AI.motivation: 传统方法通过线性分类器或视觉检查评估预训练模型，难以清晰比较不同方法的表示流形差异，需更直接的分析框架。
AI.method: 通过依次注入白噪声和PGD对抗攻击，跟踪数据点移动距离及相邻扰动间的距离变化，结合这些指标构建RMQM度量体系。
AI.result: 自监督方法生成的流形在扰动下表现出更大且稳定的位移变化，RMQM值与下游任务性能呈正相关。
AI.conclusion: RMQM能有效表征表示流形质量，为预训练方法的评估提供了新视角，且其值可预测下游任务表现。

search_text: Manifold Characteristics That Predict Downstream Task Performance 预测下游任务绩效的流形特征 Pretraining methods are typically compared by evaluating the accuracy of linear classifiers, transfer learning performance, or visually inspecting the representation manifold's (RM) lower-dimensional projections. We show that the differences between methods can be understood more clearly by investigating the RM directly, which allows for a more detailed comparison. To this end, we propose a framework and new metric to measure and compare different RMs. We also investigate and report on the RM characteristics for various pretraining methods. These characteristics are measured by applying sequentially larger local alterations to the input data, using white noise injections and Projected Gradient Descent (PGD) adversarial attacks, and then tracking each datapoint. We calculate the total distance moved for each datapoint and the relative change in distance between successive alterations. We show that self-supervised methods learn an RM where alterations lead to large but constant size changes, indicating a smoother RM than fully supervised methods. We then combine these measurements into one metric, the Representation Manifold Quality Metric (RMQM), where larger values indicate larger and less variable step sizes, and show that RMQM correlates positively with performance on downstream tasks. 通常通过评估线性分类器的准确性、迁移学习性能或目视检查表示流形 (RM) 的低维投影来比较预训练方法。我们表明，通过直接研究 RM 可以更清楚地理解方法之间的差异，从而可以进行更详细的比较。为此，我们提出了一个框架和新指标来衡量和比较不同的 RM。我们还调查并报告了各种预训练方法的 RM 特征。这些特征是通过对输入数据连续进行较大的局部更改、使用白噪声注入和投影梯度下降 (PGD) 对抗性攻击，然后跟踪每个数据点来测量的。我们计算每个数据点移动的总距离以及连续更改之间距离的相对变化。我们表明，自监督方法学习 RM，其中更改会导致较大但恒定的大小变化，这表明 RM 比完全监督方法更平滑。然后，我们将这些测量结果组合成一个指标，即表示流形质量指标 (RMQM)，其中较大的值表示较大且变化较小的步长，并表明 RMQM 与下游任务的性能呈正相关。 本文提出了一种新的度量方法（RMQM），用于直接分析预训练方法的表示流形（RM）特性，并将其与下游任务性能相关联。 Ruan van der Merwe,Gregory Newman,Etienne Barnard cs.LG,cs.CV

authors_text: Ján Koloda,Jürgen Seiler,André Kaup,Victoria Sánchez,Antonio M. Peinado
title: Frequency selective extrapolation with residual filtering for image error concealment
title_zh: 带有残差滤波的频率选择性外推用于图像错误隐藏
abstract: The purpose of signal extrapolation is to estimate unknown signal parts from known samples. This task is especially important for error concealment in image and video communication. For obtaining a high quality reconstruction, assumptions have to be made about the underlying signal in order to solve this underdetermined problem. Among existent reconstruction algorithms, frequency selective extrapolation (FSE) achieves high performance by assuming that image signals can be sparsely represented in the frequency domain. However, FSE does not take into account the low-pass behaviour of natural images. In this paper, we propose a modified FSE that takes this prior knowledge into account for the modelling, yielding significant PSNR gains.
summary: The purpose of signal extrapolation is to estimate unknown signal parts from known samples. This task is especially important for error concealment in image and video communication. For obtaining a high quality reconstruction, assumptions have to be made about the underlying signal in order to solve this underdetermined problem. Among existent reconstruction algorithms, frequency selective extrapolation (FSE) achieves high performance by assuming that image signals can be sparsely represented in the frequency domain. However, FSE does not take into account the low-pass behaviour of natural images. In this paper, we propose a modified FSE that takes this prior knowledge into account for the modelling, yielding significant PSNR gains.
abstract_zh: 信号外推的目的是从已知样本中估计未知信号部分。这项任务对于图像和视频通信中的错误隐藏尤其重要。为了获得高质量的重建，必须对底层信号做出假设，以解决这个不确定的问题。在现有的重建算法中，频率选择性外推（FSE）通过假设图像信号可以在频域中稀疏表示来实现高性能。然而，FSE 没有考虑自然图像的低通行为。在本文中，我们提出了一种改进的 FSE，在建模时考虑了先验知识，从而产生显着的 PSNR 增益。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种改进的频率选择性外推算法，通过引入残差滤波器提升图像错误掩盖效果。
AI.motivation: 现有频率选择性外推（FSE）方法未考虑自然图像的低通特性，导致重建质量受限。
AI.method: 在FSE框架中增加残差滤波模块，利用自然图像的低通先验特性优化信号建模过程。
AI.result: 实验表明改进方法在PSNR指标上取得显著提升，验证了低通特性建模的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 结合信号稀疏性与低通特性的混合模型能有效提升图像错误掩盖性能。

authors_text: Ziming Wang,Shuang Lian,Yuhao Zhang,Xiaoxin Cui,Rui Yan,Huajin Tang
title: Towards Lossless ANN-SNN Conversion under Ultra-Low Latency with Dual-Phase Optimization
title_zh: 通过双相优化实现超低延迟下的无损 ANN-SNN 转换
comment: Accepted by IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems (TNNLS)
comments: Accepted by IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems (TNNLS)
abstract: Spiking neural networks (SNNs) operating with asynchronous discrete events show higher energy efficiency with sparse computation. A popular approach for implementing deep SNNs is ANN-SNN conversion combining both efficient training of ANNs and efficient inference of SNNs. However, the accuracy loss is usually non-negligible, especially under a few time steps, which restricts the applications of SNN on latency-sensitive edge devices greatly. In this paper, we first identify that such performance degradation stems from the misrepresentation of the negative or overflow residual membrane potential in SNNs. Inspired by this, we decompose the conversion error into three parts: quantization error, clipping error, and residual membrane potential representation error. With such insights, we propose a two-stage conversion algorithm to minimize those errors respectively. Besides, We show each stage achieves significant performance gains in a complementary manner. By evaluating on challenging datasets including CIFAR-10, CIFAR- 100 and ImageNet, the proposed method demonstrates the state-of-the-art performance in terms of accuracy, latency and energy preservation. Furthermore, our method is evaluated using a more challenging object detection task, revealing notable gains in regression performance under ultra-low latency when compared to existing spike-based detection algorithms. Codes are available at https://github.com/Windere/snn-cvt-dual-phase.
summary: Spiking neural networks (SNNs) operating with asynchronous discrete events show higher energy efficiency with sparse computation. A popular approach for implementing deep SNNs is ANN-SNN conversion combining both efficient training of ANNs and efficient inference of SNNs. However, the accuracy loss is usually non-negligible, especially under a few time steps, which restricts the applications of SNN on latency-sensitive edge devices greatly. In this paper, we first identify that such performance degradation stems from the misrepresentation of the negative or overflow residual membrane potential in SNNs. Inspired by this, we decompose the conversion error into three parts: quantization error, clipping error, and residual membrane potential representation error. With such insights, we propose a two-stage conversion algorithm to minimize those errors respectively. Besides, We show each stage achieves significant performance gains in a complementary manner. By evaluating on challenging datasets including CIFAR-10, CIFAR- 100 and ImageNet, the proposed method demonstrates the state-of-the-art performance in terms of accuracy, latency and energy preservation. Furthermore, our method is evaluated using a more challenging object detection task, revealing notable gains in regression performance under ultra-low latency when compared to existing spike-based detection algorithms. Codes are available at https://github.com/Windere/snn-cvt-dual-phase.
abstract_zh: 使用异步离散事件运行的尖峰神经网络 (SNN) 通过稀疏计算表现出更高的能源效率。实现深度 SNN 的一种流行方法是结合 ANN 的高效训练和 SNN 的高效推理的 ANN-SNN 转换。然而，精度损失通常是不可忽略的，尤其是在几个时间步长的情况下，这极大地限制了 SNN 在延迟敏感的边缘设备上的应用。在本文中，我们首先发现这种性能下降源于 SNN 中负膜电位或溢出残余膜电位的错误表示。受此启发，我们将转换误差分解为三部分：量化误差、限幅误差和残余膜电位表示误差。有了这些见解，我们提出了一种两阶段转换算法来分别最小化这些错误。此外，我们表明每个阶段都以互补的方式实现了显着的性能提升。通过对 CIFAR-10、CIFAR-100 和 ImageNet 等具有挑战性的数据集进行评估，所提出的方法在准确性、延迟和能量保存方面展示了最先进的性能。此外，我们的方法使用更具挑战性的对象检测任务进行评估，与现有的基于尖峰的检测算法相比，揭示了超低延迟下回归性能的显着提升。代码可在 https://github.com/Windere/snn-cvt-dual-phase 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种双阶段优化算法，旨在解决脉冲神经网络（SNN）在超低延迟下转换时的精度损失问题，实现无损的ANN-SNN转换。
AI.motivation: 现有ANN-SNN转换方法在超低时间步长下存在显著精度损失，主要由于SNN中负值或溢出膜电位残留的表示误差未被有效处理。
AI.method: 将转换误差分解为量化误差、截断误差和膜电位残留表示误差三部分，设计两阶段优化算法分别最小化各误差，通过互补性提升整体性能。
AI.result: 在CIFAR-10/100和ImageNet数据集上达到SOTA精度，同时在超低延迟下实现能量效率；目标检测任务中回归性能较现有方法显著提升。
AI.conclusion: 双阶段优化有效缓解了SNN转换中的关键误差源，在保持超低延迟的同时实现了高精度和能效，为边缘设备应用提供了可行方案。

motivation: 现有ANN-SNN转换方法在超低时间步长下存在显著精度损失，主要由于SNN中负值或溢出膜电位残留的表示误差未被有效处理。
method: 将转换误差分解为量化误差、截断误差和膜电位残留表示误差三部分，设计两阶段优化算法分别最小化各误差，通过互补性提升整体性能。
result: 在CIFAR-10/100和ImageNet数据集上达到SOTA精度，同时在超低延迟下实现能量效率；目标检测任务中回归性能较现有方法显著提升。
conclusion: 双阶段优化有效缓解了SNN转换中的关键误差源，在保持超低延迟的同时实现了高精度和能效，为边缘设备应用提供了可行方案。
search_text: Towards Lossless ANN-SNN Conversion under Ultra-Low Latency with Dual-Phase Optimization 通过双相优化实现超低延迟下的无损 ANN-SNN 转换 Spiking neural networks (SNNs) operating with asynchronous discrete events show higher energy efficiency with sparse computation. A popular approach for implementing deep SNNs is ANN-SNN conversion combining both efficient training of ANNs and efficient inference of SNNs. However, the accuracy loss is usually non-negligible, especially under a few time steps, which restricts the applications of SNN on latency-sensitive edge devices greatly. In this paper, we first identify that such performance degradation stems from the misrepresentation of the negative or overflow residual membrane potential in SNNs. Inspired by this, we decompose the conversion error into three parts: quantization error, clipping error, and residual membrane potential representation error. With such insights, we propose a two-stage conversion algorithm to minimize those errors respectively. Besides, We show each stage achieves significant performance gains in a complementary manner. By evaluating on challenging datasets including CIFAR-10, CIFAR- 100 and ImageNet, the proposed method demonstrates the state-of-the-art performance in terms of accuracy, latency and energy preservation. Furthermore, our method is evaluated using a more challenging object detection task, revealing notable gains in regression performance under ultra-low latency when compared to existing spike-based detection algorithms. Codes are available at https://github.com/Windere/snn-cvt-dual-phase. 使用异步离散事件运行的尖峰神经网络 (SNN) 通过稀疏计算表现出更高的能源效率。实现深度 SNN 的一种流行方法是结合 ANN 的高效训练和 SNN 的高效推理的 ANN-SNN 转换。然而，精度损失通常是不可忽略的，尤其是在几个时间步长的情况下，这极大地限制了 SNN 在延迟敏感的边缘设备上的应用。在本文中，我们首先发现这种性能下降源于 SNN 中负膜电位或溢出残余膜电位的错误表示。受此启发，我们将转换误差分解为三部分：量化误差、限幅误差和残余膜电位表示误差。有了这些见解，我们提出了一种两阶段转换算法来分别最小化这些错误。此外，我们表明每个阶段都以互补的方式实现了显着的性能提升。通过对 CIFAR-10、CIFAR-100 和 ImageNet 等具有挑战性的数据集进行评估，所提出的方法在准确性、延迟和能量保存方面展示了最先进的性能。此外，我们的方法使用更具挑战性的对象检测任务进行评估，与现有的基于尖峰的检测算法相比，揭示了超低延迟下回归性能的显着提升。代码可在 https://github.com/Windere/snn-cvt-dual-phase 获取。 本文提出一种双阶段优化算法，旨在解决脉冲神经网络（SNN）在超低延迟下转换时的精度损失问题，实现无损的ANN-SNN转换。 Ziming Wang,Shuang Lian,Yuhao Zhang,Xiaoxin Cui,Rui Yan,Huajin Tang cs.NE,cs.LG

title: Adaptive Convolutional Dictionary Network for CT Metal Artifact Reduction
title_zh: 用于减少 CT 金属伪影的自适应卷积字典网络
abstract: Inspired by the great success of deep neural networks, learning-based methods have gained promising performances for metal artifact reduction (MAR) in computed tomography (CT) images. However, most of the existing approaches put less emphasis on modelling and embedding the intrinsic prior knowledge underlying this specific MAR task into their network designs. Against this issue, we propose an adaptive convolutional dictionary network (ACDNet), which leverages both model-based and learning-based methods. Specifically, we explore the prior structures of metal artifacts, e.g., non-local repetitive streaking patterns, and encode them as an explicit weighted convolutional dictionary model. Then, a simple-yet-effective algorithm is carefully designed to solve the model. By unfolding every iterative substep of the proposed algorithm into a network module, we explicitly embed the prior structure into a deep network, \emph{i.e.,} a clear interpretability for the MAR task. Furthermore, our ACDNet can automatically learn the prior for artifact-free CT images via training data and adaptively adjust the representation kernels for each input CT image based on its content. Hence, our method inherits the clear interpretability of model-based methods and maintains the powerful representation ability of learning-based methods. Comprehensive experiments executed on synthetic and clinical datasets show the superiority of our ACDNet in terms of effectiveness and model generalization. {\color{blue}{\textit{Code is available at {\url{https://github.com/hongwang01/ACDNet}.}}}}
summary: Inspired by the great success of deep neural networks, learning-based methods have gained promising performances for metal artifact reduction (MAR) in computed tomography (CT) images. However, most of the existing approaches put less emphasis on modelling and embedding the intrinsic prior knowledge underlying this specific MAR task into their network designs. Against this issue, we propose an adaptive convolutional dictionary network (ACDNet), which leverages both model-based and learning-based methods. Specifically, we explore the prior structures of metal artifacts, e.g., non-local repetitive streaking patterns, and encode them as an explicit weighted convolutional dictionary model. Then, a simple-yet-effective algorithm is carefully designed to solve the model. By unfolding every iterative substep of the proposed algorithm into a network module, we explicitly embed the prior structure into a deep network, \emph{i.e.,} a clear interpretability for the MAR task. Furthermore, our ACDNet can automatically learn the prior for artifact-free CT images via training data and adaptively adjust the representation kernels for each input CT image based on its content. Hence, our method inherits the clear interpretability of model-based methods and maintains the powerful representation ability of learning-based methods. Comprehensive experiments executed on synthetic and clinical datasets show the superiority of our ACDNet in terms of effectiveness and model generalization. {\color{blue}{\textit{Code is available at {\url{https://github.com/hongwang01/ACDNet}.}}}}
abstract_zh: 受深度神经网络巨大成功的启发，基于学习的方法在计算机断层扫描（CT）图像中的金属伪影减少（MAR）方面取得了令人鼓舞的性能。然而，大多数现有方法不太重视建模和将特定 MAR 任务背后的内在先验知识嵌入到网络设计中。针对这个问题，我们提出了一种自适应卷积字典网络（ACDNet），它利用基于模型和基于学习的方法。具体来说，我们探索金属制品的先验结构，例如非局部重复条纹模式，并将它们编码为显式加权卷积字典模型。然后，精心设计一个简单而有效的算法来求解模型。通过将所提出算法的每个迭代子步骤展开到网络模块中，我们明确地将先验结构嵌入到深层网络中，emph{即}对 MAR 任务具有清晰的可解释性。此外，我们的 ACDNet 可以通过训练数据自动学习无伪影 CT 图像的先验，并根据每个输入 CT 图像的内容自适应调整表示内核。因此，我们的方法继承了基于模型的方法的清晰可解释性，并保持了基于学习的方法的强大表示能力。在合成和临床数据集上执行的综合实验显示了我们的 ACDNet 在有效性和模型泛化方面的优越性。 {\color{blue}{\textit{代码可在 {\url{https://github.com/hongwang01/ACDNet} 获取。}}}}

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种自适应卷积字典网络（ACDNet），用于减少CT图像中的金属伪影。
AI.motivation: 现有金属伪影减少方法未能充分结合任务先验知识，导致模型可解释性与学习能力不足。
AI.method: 设计融合模型先验知识的卷积字典网络，通过展开迭代子步骤构建可解释网络模块，并自适应调整卷积核以适应输入图像内容。
AI.result: 在合成与临床数据集上均表现出优于现有方法的去伪影效果及模型泛化能力。
AI.conclusion: ACDNet通过结合模型驱动与数据驱动方法，在保持可解释性的同时提升了金属伪影减少性能。

title: $q$-Munchausen Reinforcement Learning
title_zh: $q$-Munchausen 强化学习
abstract: The recently successful Munchausen Reinforcement Learning (M-RL) features implicit Kullback-Leibler (KL) regularization by augmenting the reward function with logarithm of the current stochastic policy. Though significant improvement has been shown with the Boltzmann softmax policy, when the Tsallis sparsemax policy is considered, the augmentation leads to a flat learning curve for almost every problem considered. We show that it is due to the mismatch between the conventional logarithm and the non-logarithmic (generalized) nature of Tsallis entropy. Drawing inspiration from the Tsallis statistics literature, we propose to correct the mismatch of M-RL with the help of $q$-logarithm/exponential functions. The proposed formulation leads to implicit Tsallis KL regularization under the maximum Tsallis entropy framework. We show such formulation of M-RL again achieves superior performance on benchmark problems and sheds light on more general M-RL with various entropic indices $q$.
summary: The recently successful Munchausen Reinforcement Learning (M-RL) features implicit Kullback-Leibler (KL) regularization by augmenting the reward function with logarithm of the current stochastic policy. Though significant improvement has been shown with the Boltzmann softmax policy, when the Tsallis sparsemax policy is considered, the augmentation leads to a flat learning curve for almost every problem considered. We show that it is due to the mismatch between the conventional logarithm and the non-logarithmic (generalized) nature of Tsallis entropy. Drawing inspiration from the Tsallis statistics literature, we propose to correct the mismatch of M-RL with the help of $q$-logarithm/exponential functions. The proposed formulation leads to implicit Tsallis KL regularization under the maximum Tsallis entropy framework. We show such formulation of M-RL again achieves superior performance on benchmark problems and sheds light on more general M-RL with various entropic indices $q$.
abstract_zh: 最近成功的孟乔森强化学习（M-RL）通过用当前随机策略的对数增强奖励函数来实现隐式 Kullback-Leibler（KL）正则化。尽管 Boltzmann softmax 策略已显示出显着的改进，但当考虑 Tsallis Sparsemax 策略时，增强会导致几乎每个考虑的问题都平坦的学习曲线。我们证明这是由于传统对数和 Tsallis 熵的非对数（广义）性质之间的不匹配造成的。从 Tsallis 统计文献中汲取灵感，我们建议借助 $q$-对数/指数函数来纠正 M-RL 的不匹配。所提出的公式导致最大 Tsallis 熵框架下的隐式 Tsallis KL 正则化。我们证明 M-RL 的这种表述再次在基准问题上实现了卓越的性能，并揭示了具有各种熵指数 $q$ 的更一般的 M-RL。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于$q$-对数函数的Munchausen强化学习方法，以解决Tsallis熵框架下原有方法性能下降的问题。
AI.motivation: 传统Munchausen RL在使用Tsallis稀疏max策略时出现学习曲线平坦化现象，源于对数函数与Tsallis熵非对数特性的不匹配。
AI.method: 引入Tsallis统计中的$q$-对数/指数函数修正奖励函数增广方式，建立隐式Tsallis KL正则化框架。
AI.result: 新方法在基准任务中恢复了优越性能，并展示了不同熵指数$q$下M-RL的扩展可能性。
AI.conclusion: 通过修正熵函数匹配性，本文改进了Tsallis框架下的M-RL性能，为广义熵正则化提供了新思路。

authors_text: Haozhe Liu,Haoqin Ji,Yuexiang Li,Nanjun He,Haoqian Wu,Feng Liu,Linlin Shen,Yefeng Zheng
title: Robust Representation via Dynamic Feature Aggregation
title_zh: 通过动态特征聚合的鲁棒表示
abstract: Deep convolutional neural network (CNN) based models are vulnerable to the adversarial attacks. One of the possible reasons is that the embedding space of CNN based model is sparse, resulting in a large space for the generation of adversarial samples. In this study, we propose a method, denoted as Dynamic Feature Aggregation, to compress the embedding space with a novel regularization. Particularly, the convex combination between two samples are regarded as the pivot for aggregation. In the embedding space, the selected samples are guided to be similar to the representation of the pivot. On the other side, to mitigate the trivial solution of such regularization, the last fully-connected layer of the model is replaced by an orthogonal classifier, in which the embedding codes for different classes are processed orthogonally and separately. With the regularization and orthogonal classifier, a more compact embedding space can be obtained, which accordingly improves the model robustness against adversarial attacks. An averaging accuracy of 56.91% is achieved by our method on CIFAR-10 against various attack methods, which significantly surpasses a solid baseline (Mixup) by a margin of 37.31%. More surprisingly, empirical results show that, the proposed method can also achieve the state-of-the-art performance for out-of-distribution (OOD) detection, due to the learned compact feature space. An F1 score of 0.937 is achieved by the proposed method, when adopting CIFAR-10 as in-distribution (ID) dataset and LSUN as OOD dataset. Code is available at https://github.com/HaozheLiu-ST/DynamicFeatureAggregation.
summary: Deep convolutional neural network (CNN) based models are vulnerable to the adversarial attacks. One of the possible reasons is that the embedding space of CNN based model is sparse, resulting in a large space for the generation of adversarial samples. In this study, we propose a method, denoted as Dynamic Feature Aggregation, to compress the embedding space with a novel regularization. Particularly, the convex combination between two samples are regarded as the pivot for aggregation. In the embedding space, the selected samples are guided to be similar to the representation of the pivot. On the other side, to mitigate the trivial solution of such regularization, the last fully-connected layer of the model is replaced by an orthogonal classifier, in which the embedding codes for different classes are processed orthogonally and separately. With the regularization and orthogonal classifier, a more compact embedding space can be obtained, which accordingly improves the model robustness against adversarial attacks. An averaging accuracy of 56.91% is achieved by our method on CIFAR-10 against various attack methods, which significantly surpasses a solid baseline (Mixup) by a margin of 37.31%. More surprisingly, empirical results show that, the proposed method can also achieve the state-of-the-art performance for out-of-distribution (OOD) detection, due to the learned compact feature space. An F1 score of 0.937 is achieved by the proposed method, when adopting CIFAR-10 as in-distribution (ID) dataset and LSUN as OOD dataset. Code is available at https://github.com/HaozheLiu-ST/DynamicFeatureAggregation.
abstract_zh: 基于深度卷积神经网络（CNN）的模型很容易受到对抗性攻击。可能的原因之一是基于CNN的模型的嵌入空间稀疏，导致生成对抗性样本的空间很大。在本研究中，我们提出了一种称为动态特征聚合的方法，通过新颖的正则化来压缩嵌入空间。特别地，两个样本之间的凸组合被视为聚合的枢轴。在嵌入空间中，所选样本被引导为类似于枢轴的表示。另一方面，为了减轻这种正则化的琐碎解决方案，模型的最后一个全连接层被正交分类器取代，其中不同类的嵌入代码被正交且单独地处理。通过正则化和正交分类器，可以获得更紧凑的嵌入空间，从而提高模型针对对抗性攻击的鲁棒性。我们的方法在 CIFAR-10 上针对各种攻击方法实现了 56.91% 的平均准确率，显着超过了可靠基线（Mixup）37.31%。更令人惊讶的是，经验结果表明，由于学习到的紧凑特征空间，所提出的方法还可以实现分布外（OOD）检测的最先进性能。当采用 CIFAR-10 作为分布内 (ID) 数据集和 LSUN 作为 OOD 数据集时，所提出的方法获得了 0.937 的 F1 分数。代码可在 https://github.com/HaozheLiu-ST/DynamicFeatureAggregation 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出动态特征聚合方法，通过压缩嵌入空间提升深度卷积神经网络对抗对抗攻击的鲁棒性。
AI.motivation: 基于CNN的模型因嵌入空间稀疏性易受对抗攻击，需通过压缩嵌入空间减少对抗样本生成的可能性。
AI.method: 采用动态特征聚合技术，以样本凸组合为聚合基准，并用正交分类器替代全连接层，实现嵌入空间的正交处理。
AI.result: 在CIFAR-10数据集上对抗攻击准确率达56.91%，较基准方法提升37.31%；在OOD检测中F1分数达0.937。
AI.conclusion: 动态特征聚合通过紧凑特征学习显著提升模型鲁棒性及分布外检测性能。

authors_text: Weili Nie,Brandon Guo,Yujia Huang,Chaowei Xiao,Arash Vahdat,Anima Anandkumar
title: Diffusion Models for Adversarial Purification
title_zh: 对抗性净化的扩散模型
abstract: Adversarial purification refers to a class of defense methods that remove adversarial perturbations using a generative model. These methods do not make assumptions on the form of attack and the classification model, and thus can defend pre-existing classifiers against unseen threats. However, their performance currently falls behind adversarial training methods. In this work, we propose DiffPure that uses diffusion models for adversarial purification: Given an adversarial example, we first diffuse it with a small amount of noise following a forward diffusion process, and then recover the clean image through a reverse generative process. To evaluate our method against strong adaptive attacks in an efficient and scalable way, we propose to use the adjoint method to compute full gradients of the reverse generative process. Extensive experiments on three image datasets including CIFAR-10, ImageNet and CelebA-HQ with three classifier architectures including ResNet, WideResNet and ViT demonstrate that our method achieves the state-of-the-art results, outperforming current adversarial training and adversarial purification methods, often by a large margin. Project page: https://diffpure.github.io.
summary: Adversarial purification refers to a class of defense methods that remove adversarial perturbations using a generative model. These methods do not make assumptions on the form of attack and the classification model, and thus can defend pre-existing classifiers against unseen threats. However, their performance currently falls behind adversarial training methods. In this work, we propose DiffPure that uses diffusion models for adversarial purification: Given an adversarial example, we first diffuse it with a small amount of noise following a forward diffusion process, and then recover the clean image through a reverse generative process. To evaluate our method against strong adaptive attacks in an efficient and scalable way, we propose to use the adjoint method to compute full gradients of the reverse generative process. Extensive experiments on three image datasets including CIFAR-10, ImageNet and CelebA-HQ with three classifier architectures including ResNet, WideResNet and ViT demonstrate that our method achieves the state-of-the-art results, outperforming current adversarial training and adversarial purification methods, often by a large margin. Project page: https://diffpure.github.io.
abstract_zh: 对抗性净化是指使用生成模型消除对抗性扰动的一类防御方法。这些方法不对攻击形式和分类模型做出假设，因此可以保护预先存在的分类器免受看不见的威胁。然而，他们的表现目前落后于对抗性训练方法。在这项工作中，我们提出了使用扩散模型进行对抗性净化的 DiffPure：给定一个对抗性示例，我们首先在前向扩散过程中用少量噪声对其进行扩散，然后通过反向生成过程恢复干净的图像。为了以有效且可扩展的方式评估我们的方法对抗强自适应攻击的能力，我们建议使用伴随方法来计算反向生成过程的完整梯度。在 CIFAR-10、ImageNet 和 CelebA-HQ 等三个图像数据集上以及 ResNet、WideResNet 和 ViT 等三种分类器架构上进行的广泛实验表明，我们的方法取得了最先进的结果，远远优于当前的对抗训练和对抗净化方法。项目页面：https://diffpure.github.io。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于扩散模型的对抗净化方法DiffPure，通过正向扩散过程添加噪声并利用反向生成过程恢复干净图像，以移除对抗样本中的扰动。
AI.motivation: 现有对抗净化方法在防御未知攻击时效果不佳，而对抗训练方法虽有效但依赖特定模型假设。需开发一种无需攻击形式假设且性能更优的通用防御方法。
AI.method: 采用扩散模型框架：首先通过正向扩散过程向对抗样本注入少量噪声，再通过反向生成过程恢复干净图像；使用伴随方法高效计算反向生成过程的梯度以应对强适应性攻击。
AI.result: 在CIFAR-10、ImageNet和CelebA-HQ三个数据集上，与ResNet、WideResNet和ViT三种分类器结合，DiffPure在对抗净化任务中达到最先进水平，显著优于现有对抗训练和净化方法。
AI.conclusion: DiffPure通过扩散模型的生成能力实现了高效且通用的对抗净化，验证了扩散模型在安全领域应用的潜力，为对抗样本防御提供了新思路。

title: PRISM: Pre-trained Indeterminate Speaker Representation Model for Speaker Diarization and Speaker Verification
title_zh: PRISM：用于说话人分类和说话人验证的预训练不确定说话人表示模型
abstract: Speaker embedding has been a fundamental feature for speaker-related tasks such as verification, clustering, and diarization. Traditionally, speaker embeddings are represented as fixed vectors in high-dimensional space. This could lead to biased estimations, especially when handling shorter utterances. In this paper we propose to represent a speaker utterance as "floating" vector whose state is indeterminate without knowing the context. The state of a speaker representation is jointly determined by itself, other speech from the same speaker, as well as other speakers it is being compared to. The content of the speech also contributes to determining the final state of a speaker representation. We pre-train an indeterminate speaker representation model that estimates the state of an utterance based on the context. The pre-trained model can be fine-tuned for downstream tasks such as speaker verification, speaker clustering, and speaker diarization. Substantial improvements are observed across all downstream tasks.
summary: Speaker embedding has been a fundamental feature for speaker-related tasks such as verification, clustering, and diarization. Traditionally, speaker embeddings are represented as fixed vectors in high-dimensional space. This could lead to biased estimations, especially when handling shorter utterances. In this paper we propose to represent a speaker utterance as "floating" vector whose state is indeterminate without knowing the context. The state of a speaker representation is jointly determined by itself, other speech from the same speaker, as well as other speakers it is being compared to. The content of the speech also contributes to determining the final state of a speaker representation. We pre-train an indeterminate speaker representation model that estimates the state of an utterance based on the context. The pre-trained model can be fine-tuned for downstream tasks such as speaker verification, speaker clustering, and speaker diarization. Substantial improvements are observed across all downstream tasks.
abstract_zh: 说话人嵌​​入一直是与说话人相关的任务（例如验证、聚类和二值化）的基本特征。传统上，说话人嵌入被表示为高维空间中的固定向量。这可能会导致估计有偏差，尤其是在处理较短的话语时。在本文中，我们建议将说话者的话语表示为“浮动”向量，其状态在不知道上下文的情况下是不确定的。说话者表示的状态由其自身、来自同一说话者的其他语音以及与其进行比较的其他说话者共同确定。演讲的内容也有助于确定演讲者表示的最终状态。我们预先训练一个不确定的说话人表示模型，该模型根据上下文估计话语的状态。预训练模型可以针对说话人验证、说话人聚类和说话人二值化等下游任务进行微调。在所有下游任务中都观察到了显着的改进。

AI.tldr: 本文提出PRISM模型，通过预训练不确定说话人表示方法，提升说话人验证和聚类任务的性能。
AI.motivation: 传统固定向量表示在处理短语音时存在偏差，且无法有效捕捉上下文相关性。
AI.method: 构建浮动向量表示，联合考虑说话人自身、同源语音、对比语音及内容信息，预训练模型估计上下文相关状态。
AI.result: 在说话人验证、聚类和分割任务中均取得显著性能提升。
AI.conclusion: PRISM模型通过引入上下文感知的不确定表示，为说话人相关任务提供了更鲁棒的解决方案。

search_text: PRISM: Pre-trained Indeterminate Speaker Representation Model for Speaker Diarization and Speaker Verification PRISM：用于说话人分类和说话人验证的预训练不确定说话人表示模型 Speaker embedding has been a fundamental feature for speaker-related tasks such as verification, clustering, and diarization. Traditionally, speaker embeddings are represented as fixed vectors in high-dimensional space. This could lead to biased estimations, especially when handling shorter utterances. In this paper we propose to represent a speaker utterance as "floating" vector whose state is indeterminate without knowing the context. The state of a speaker representation is jointly determined by itself, other speech from the same speaker, as well as other speakers it is being compared to. The content of the speech also contributes to determining the final state of a speaker representation. We pre-train an indeterminate speaker representation model that estimates the state of an utterance based on the context. The pre-trained model can be fine-tuned for downstream tasks such as speaker verification, speaker clustering, and speaker diarization. Substantial improvements are observed across all downstream tasks. 说话人嵌​​入一直是与说话人相关的任务（例如验证、聚类和二值化）的基本特征。传统上，说话人嵌入被表示为高维空间中的固定向量。这可能会导致估计有偏差，尤其是在处理较短的话语时。在本文中，我们建议将说话者的话语表示为“浮动”向量，其状态在不知道上下文的情况下是不确定的。说话者表示的状态由其自身、来自同一说话者的其他语音以及与其进行比较的其他说话者共同确定。演讲的内容也有助于确定演讲者表示的最终状态。我们预先训练一个不确定的说话人表示模型，该模型根据上下文估计话语的状态。预训练模型可以针对说话人验证、说话人聚类和说话人二值化等下游任务进行微调。在所有下游任务中都观察到了显着的改进。 本文提出PRISM模型，通过预训练不确定说话人表示方法，提升说话人验证和聚类任务的性能。 Siqi Zheng,Hongbin Suo,Qian Chen cs.SD,eess.AS

authors_text: Yen-Ting Lin,Hui-Chi Kuo,Ze-Song Xu,Ssu Chiu,Chieh-Chi Hung,Yi-Cheng Chen,Chao-Wei Huang,Yun-Nung Chen
title: Miutsu: NTU's TaskBot for the Alexa Prize
title_zh: Miutsu：NTU 的 TaskBot 荣获 Alexa 奖
abstract: This paper introduces Miutsu, National Taiwan University's Alexa Prize TaskBot, which is designed to assist users in completing tasks requiring multiple steps and decisions in two different domains -- home improvement and cooking. We overview our system design and architectural goals, and detail the proposed core elements, including question answering, task retrieval, social chatting, and various conversational modules. A dialogue flow is proposed to provide a robust and engaging conversation when handling complex tasks. We discuss the faced challenges during the competition and potential future work.
summary: This paper introduces Miutsu, National Taiwan University's Alexa Prize TaskBot, which is designed to assist users in completing tasks requiring multiple steps and decisions in two different domains -- home improvement and cooking. We overview our system design and architectural goals, and detail the proposed core elements, including question answering, task retrieval, social chatting, and various conversational modules. A dialogue flow is proposed to provide a robust and engaging conversation when handling complex tasks. We discuss the faced challenges during the competition and potential future work.
abstract_zh: 本文介绍了台湾大学 Miutsu 的 Alexa Prize TaskBot，该机器人旨在帮助用户完成家居装修和烹饪两个不同领域中需要多个步骤和决策的任务。我们概述了我们的系统设计和架构目标，并详细介绍了所提出的核心元素，包括问答、任务检索、社交聊天和各种对话模块。提出对话流以在处理复杂任务时提供稳健且引人入胜的对话。我们讨论了比赛期间面临的挑战以及未来潜在的工作。

AI.tldr: 介绍国立台湾大学为Alexa Prize开发的Miutsu TaskBot，旨在通过多步骤对话帮助用户完成家庭改善和烹饪领域的任务。
AI.motivation: 为用户提供更自然和高效的多步骤任务处理，提升语音助手在复杂场景下的实用性。
AI.method: 结合问答、任务检索、社交聊天模块及多步骤对话流程，设计模块化系统以处理复杂任务。
AI.result: 展示了系统在复杂任务处理中的有效性，并识别了技术挑战。
AI.conclusion: Miutsu TaskBot通过模块化设计和多步骤对话提升了复杂任务处理能力，未来需进一步优化对话理解和个性化。

authors_text: Thai Van Nguyen,Xincheng Dai,Ibrahim Khan,Ruck Thawonmas,Hai V. Pham
title: A Deep Reinforcement Learning Blind AI in DareFightingICE
title_zh: DareFightingICE 中的深度强化学习盲人人工智能
abstract: This paper presents a deep reinforcement learning agent (AI) that uses sound as the input on the DareFightingICE platform at the DareFightingICE Competition in IEEE CoG 2022. In this work, an AI that only uses sound as the input is called blind AI. While state-of-the-art AIs rely mostly on visual or structured observations provided by their environments, learning to play games from only sound is still new and thus challenging. We propose different approaches to process audio data and use the Proximal Policy Optimization algorithm for our blind AI. We also propose to use our blind AI in evaluation of sound designs submitted to the competition and define two metrics for this task. The experimental results show the effectiveness of not only our blind AI but also the proposed two metrics.
summary: This paper presents a deep reinforcement learning agent (AI) that uses sound as the input on the DareFightingICE platform at the DareFightingICE Competition in IEEE CoG 2022. In this work, an AI that only uses sound as the input is called blind AI. While state-of-the-art AIs rely mostly on visual or structured observations provided by their environments, learning to play games from only sound is still new and thus challenging. We propose different approaches to process audio data and use the Proximal Policy Optimization algorithm for our blind AI. We also propose to use our blind AI in evaluation of sound designs submitted to the competition and define two metrics for this task. The experimental results show the effectiveness of not only our blind AI but also the proposed two metrics.
abstract_zh: 本文在 IEEE CoG 2022 的 DareFightingICE 竞赛中提出了一种在 DareFightingICE 平台上使用声音作为输入的深度强化学习代理（AI）。在这项工作中，仅使用声音作为输入的 AI 称为盲 AI。虽然最先进的人工智能主要依赖于环境提供的视觉或结构化观察，但学习仅通过声音玩游戏仍然是新事物，因此具有挑战性。我们提出了不同的方法来处理音频数据，并为我们的盲人人工智能使用近端策略优化算法。我们还建议使用盲人工智能来评估提交给竞赛的声音设计，并为此任务定义两个指标。实验结果不仅证明了我们的盲人工智能的有效性，还证明了所提出的两个指标的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于深度强化学习的盲AI，仅使用声音作为输入在DareFightingICE平台上进行游戏，并设计了评估声音设计的两个指标。
AI.motivation: 现有AI主要依赖视觉或结构化数据，而仅通过声音输入进行游戏学习的研究较少，具有挑战性且值得探索。
AI.method: 采用音频数据处理方法结合近端策略优化算法（PPO）构建盲AI，并提出两个指标用于评估竞赛中的声音设计方案。
AI.result: 实验验证了盲AI的有效性及所提出的两个评估指标的可行性。
AI.conclusion: 盲AI和评估指标在比赛中表现良好，为基于声音的AI研究提供了新思路和工具。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于深度强化学习的盲AI，仅使用声音作为输入在DareFightingICE平台上进行游戏，并设计了评估声音设计的两个指标。

title: Behaviour Explanation via Causal Analysis of Mental States: A Preliminary Report
title_zh: 通过心理状态因果分析进行行为解释：初步报告
abstract: Inspired by a novel action-theoretic formalization of actual cause, Khan and Lespérance (2021) recently proposed a first account of causal knowledge that supports epistemic effects, models causal knowledge dynamics, and allows sensing actions to be causes of observed effects. To date, no other study has looked specifically at these issues. But their formalization is not sufficiently expressive enough to model explanations via causal analysis of mental states as it ignores a crucial aspect of theory of mind, namely motivations. In this paper, we build on their work to support causal reasoning about conative effects. In our framework, one can reason about causes of motivational states, and we allow motivation-altering actions to be causes of observed effects. We illustrate that this formalization along with a model of goal recognition can be utilized to explain agent behaviour in communicative multiagent contexts.
summary: Inspired by a novel action-theoretic formalization of actual cause, Khan and Lespérance (2021) recently proposed a first account of causal knowledge that supports epistemic effects, models causal knowledge dynamics, and allows sensing actions to be causes of observed effects. To date, no other study has looked specifically at these issues. But their formalization is not sufficiently expressive enough to model explanations via causal analysis of mental states as it ignores a crucial aspect of theory of mind, namely motivations. In this paper, we build on their work to support causal reasoning about conative effects. In our framework, one can reason about causes of motivational states, and we allow motivation-altering actions to be causes of observed effects. We illustrate that this formalization along with a model of goal recognition can be utilized to explain agent behaviour in communicative multiagent contexts.
abstract_zh: 受实际原因的新颖的行动理论形式化的启发，Khan 和 Lespérance（2021）最近提出了第一个因果知识的解释，该解释支持认知效应，对因果知识动态进行建模，并允许感知行为成为观察到的效果的原因。迄今为止，还没有其他研究专门研究过这些问题。但它们的形式化不够具有足够的表达能力，无法通过心理状态的因果分析来建模解释，因为它忽略了心理理论的一个关键方面，即动机。在本文中，我们以他们的工作为基础来支持意动效应的因果推理。在我们的框架中，人们可以推理动机状态的原因，并且我们允许改变动机的行为成为观察到的效果的原因。我们说明，这种形式化以及目标识别模型可以用来解释通信多智能体环境中的智能体行为。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在通过扩展因果分析框架，引入动机建模以解释多智能体系统中的行为。
AI.motivation: 现有因果知识框架无法充分建模心理理论中的动机因素，导致无法通过因果分析解释基于动机的行为。
AI.method: 在Khan和Lespérance的因果知识框架基础上，新增对动机状态的因果推理模块，并结合目标识别模型进行行为解释。
AI.result: 构建了支持动机改变动作的因果推理框架，实现了多智能体通信场景中行为解释的可行性验证。
AI.conclusion: 整合动机建模的因果分析框架能更准确解释智能体行为，为心理理论驱动的多智能体系统提供新方法。

ai_content.tldr: 本文旨在通过扩展因果分析框架，引入动机建模以解释多智能体系统中的行为。
ai_content.motivation: 现有因果知识框架无法充分建模心理理论中的动机因素，导致无法通过因果分析解释基于动机的行为。

ai_content.result: 构建了支持动机改变动作的因果推理框架，实现了多智能体通信场景中行为解释的可行性验证。
ai_content.conclusion: 整合动机建模的因果分析框架能更准确解释智能体行为，为心理理论驱动的多智能体系统提供新方法。

search_text: Behaviour Explanation via Causal Analysis of Mental States: A Preliminary Report 通过心理状态因果分析进行行为解释：初步报告 Inspired by a novel action-theoretic formalization of actual cause, Khan and Lespérance (2021) recently proposed a first account of causal knowledge that supports epistemic effects, models causal knowledge dynamics, and allows sensing actions to be causes of observed effects. To date, no other study has looked specifically at these issues. But their formalization is not sufficiently expressive enough to model explanations via causal analysis of mental states as it ignores a crucial aspect of theory of mind, namely motivations. In this paper, we build on their work to support causal reasoning about conative effects. In our framework, one can reason about causes of motivational states, and we allow motivation-altering actions to be causes of observed effects. We illustrate that this formalization along with a model of goal recognition can be utilized to explain agent behaviour in communicative multiagent contexts. 受实际原因的新颖的行动理论形式化的启发，Khan 和 Lespérance（2021）最近提出了第一个因果知识的解释，该解释支持认知效应，对因果知识动态进行建模，并允许感知行为成为观察到的效果的原因。迄今为止，还没有其他研究专门研究过这些问题。但它们的形式化不够具有足够的表达能力，无法通过心理状态的因果分析来建模解释，因为它忽略了心理理论的一个关键方面，即动机。在本文中，我们以他们的工作为基础来支持意动效应的因果推理。在我们的框架中，人们可以推理动机状态的原因，并且我们允许改变动机的行为成为观察到的效果的原因。我们说明，这种形式化以及目标识别模型可以用来解释通信多智能体环境中的智能体行为。 本文旨在通过扩展因果分析框架，引入动机建模以解释多智能体系统中的行为。 Shakil M. Khan cs.AI,cs.LO,cs.MA

authors_text: Hengwei Zhang,Hua Yang,Haitao Wang,Zhigang Wang,Shengmin Zhang,Ming Chen
title: Autonomous Electric Vehicle Battery Disassembly Based on NeuroSymbolic Computing
title_zh: 基于神经符号计算的自主电动汽车电池拆卸
comment: Accepted to IntelliSys 2022(Intelligent Systems Conference),15 pages with 6 figures
comments: Accepted to IntelliSys 2022(Intelligent Systems Conference),15 pages with 6 figures
abstract: The booming of electric vehicles demands efficient battery disassembly for recycling to be environment-friendly. Due to the unstructured environment and high uncertainties, battery disassembly is still primarily done by humans, probably assisted by robots. It is highly desirable to design autonomous solutions to improve work efficiency and lower human risks in high voltage and toxic environments. This paper proposes a novel framework of the NeuroSymbolic task and motion planning method to disassemble batteries in an unstructured environment using robots automatically. It enables robots to independently locate and disassemble battery bolts, with or without obstacles. This study not only provides a solution for intelligently disassembling electric vehicle batteries but also verifies its feasibility through a set of test results with the robot accomplishing the disassembly tasks in a complex and dynamic environment.
summary: The booming of electric vehicles demands efficient battery disassembly for recycling to be environment-friendly. Due to the unstructured environment and high uncertainties, battery disassembly is still primarily done by humans, probably assisted by robots. It is highly desirable to design autonomous solutions to improve work efficiency and lower human risks in high voltage and toxic environments. This paper proposes a novel framework of the NeuroSymbolic task and motion planning method to disassemble batteries in an unstructured environment using robots automatically. It enables robots to independently locate and disassemble battery bolts, with or without obstacles. This study not only provides a solution for intelligently disassembling electric vehicle batteries but also verifies its feasibility through a set of test results with the robot accomplishing the disassembly tasks in a complex and dynamic environment.
abstract_zh: 电动汽车的蓬勃发展需要高效的电池拆解和回收，以实现环保。由于环境非结构化和高度不确定性，电池拆卸仍然主要由人类完成，可能由机器人辅助。非常需要设计自主解决方案来提高工作效率并降低高压和有毒环境中的人员风险。本文提出了一种新颖的 NeuroSymbolic 任务框架和运动规划方法，以使用机器人在非结构化环境中自动拆卸电池。它使机器人能够在有或没有障碍物的情况下独立定位和拆卸电池螺栓。该研究不仅为智能拆卸电动汽车电池提供了解决方案，而且通过机器人在复杂动态环境下完成拆卸任务的一组测试结果验证了其可行性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于神经符号计算的自主电动汽车电池拆解框架，使机器人能在复杂环境中自动定位并拆解电池螺栓。
AI.motivation: 电动汽车电池回收需要高效且安全的拆解方案，但传统人工或机器人辅助方式存在效率低、高风险等问题，亟需自主化解决方案。
AI.method: 设计神经符号任务与运动规划方法，结合符号推理与深度学习，实现机器人对电池螺栓的自主定位与拆解，适应有无障碍物的复杂环境。
AI.result: 实验验证机器人可在动态复杂环境中完成电池拆解任务，证明了该方法的可行性与有效性。
AI.conclusion: 提出的神经符号框架为电动汽车电池智能化拆解提供了可行方案，有助于提升回收效率并降低人工风险。

title: ReDFeat: Recoupling Detection and Description for Multimodal Feature Learning
title_zh: ReDFeat：多模态特征学习的重新耦合检测和描述
abstract: Deep-learning-based local feature extraction algorithms that combine detection and description have made significant progress in visible image matching. However, the end-to-end training of such frameworks is notoriously unstable due to the lack of strong supervision of detection and the inappropriate coupling between detection and description. The problem is magnified in cross-modal scenarios, in which most methods heavily rely on the pre-training. In this paper, we recouple independent constraints of detection and description of multimodal feature learning with a mutual weighting strategy, in which the detected probabilities of robust features are forced to peak and repeat, while features with high detection scores are emphasized during optimization. Different from previous works, those weights are detached from back propagation so that the detected probability of indistinct features would not be directly suppressed and the training would be more stable. Moreover, we propose the Super Detector, a detector that possesses a large receptive field and is equipped with learnable non-maximum suppression layers, to fulfill the harsh terms of detection. Finally, we build a benchmark that contains cross visible, infrared, near-infrared and synthetic aperture radar image pairs for evaluating the performance of features in feature matching and image registration tasks. Extensive experiments demonstrate that features trained with the recoulped detection and description, named ReDFeat, surpass previous state-of-the-arts in the benchmark, while the model can be readily trained from scratch.
summary: Deep-learning-based local feature extraction algorithms that combine detection and description have made significant progress in visible image matching. However, the end-to-end training of such frameworks is notoriously unstable due to the lack of strong supervision of detection and the inappropriate coupling between detection and description. The problem is magnified in cross-modal scenarios, in which most methods heavily rely on the pre-training. In this paper, we recouple independent constraints of detection and description of multimodal feature learning with a mutual weighting strategy, in which the detected probabilities of robust features are forced to peak and repeat, while features with high detection scores are emphasized during optimization. Different from previous works, those weights are detached from back propagation so that the detected probability of indistinct features would not be directly suppressed and the training would be more stable. Moreover, we propose the Super Detector, a detector that possesses a large receptive field and is equipped with learnable non-maximum suppression layers, to fulfill the harsh terms of detection. Finally, we build a benchmark that contains cross visible, infrared, near-infrared and synthetic aperture radar image pairs for evaluating the performance of features in feature matching and image registration tasks. Extensive experiments demonstrate that features trained with the recoulped detection and description, named ReDFeat, surpass previous state-of-the-arts in the benchmark, while the model can be readily trained from scratch.
abstract_zh: 基于深度学习的结合检测和描述的局部特征提取算法在可见图像匹配方面取得了重大进展。然而，由于缺乏对检测的强有力监督以及检测和描述之间的不适当耦合，此类框架的端到端训练非常不稳定。在跨模式场景中，这个问题会被放大，其中大多数方法严重依赖预训练。在本文中，我们通过相互加权策略重新耦合多模态特征学习的检测和描述的独立约束，其中鲁棒特征的检测概率被迫达到峰值并重复，而在优化过程中强调检测分数高的特征。与之前的工作不同，这些权重与反向传播分离，因此检测到的不明确特征的概率不会被直接抑制，训练会更加稳定。此外，我们提出了超级检测器，一种具有大感受野并配备可学习非极大值抑制层的检测器，以满足严格的检测条件。最后，我们建立了一个包含交叉可见光、红外、近红外和合成孔径雷达图像对的基准，用于评估特征匹配和图像配准任务中特征的性能。大量实验表明，使用名为 ReDFeat 的重新检测和描述训练的特征超越了基准测试中之前的最先进技术，同时该模型可以轻松地从头开始训练。

AI.tldr: 本文提出ReDFeat方法，通过解耦检测与描述的约束策略，提升多模态特征学习在跨模态图像匹配中的性能。
AI.motivation: 现有端到端框架因检测与描述耦合不当导致训练不稳定，尤其在跨模态场景中依赖预训练，难以满足检测与描述的独立约束需求。
AI.method: 采用互惠加权策略解耦检测与描述，通过分离权重避免反向传播干扰；设计具有大感受野和可学习非极大值抑制层的Super Detector，并构建包含可见光、红外等多模态图像对的基准数据集。
AI.result: ReDFeat在基准测试中超越现有最先进方法，且模型可从零开始训练无需预训练。
AI.conclusion: 解耦检测与描述的约束并结合新型检测器设计，有效提升了跨模态特征学习的稳定性与匹配性能。

Moreover, we propose the Super Detector, a detector that possesses a large receptive field and is equipped with learnable non-maximum suppression layers, to fulfill the harsh terms of detection. Finally, we build a benchmark that contains cross visible, infrared, near-infrared and synthetic aperture radar image pairs for evaluating the performance of features in feature matching and image registration tasks. Extensive experiments demonstrate that features trained with the recoulped detection and description, named ReDFeat, surpass previous state-of-the-arts in the benchmark, while the model can be readily trained from scratch. 基于深度学习的结合检测和描述的局部特征提取算法在可见图像匹配方面取得了重大进展。然而，由于缺乏对检测的强有力监督以及检测和描述之间的不适当耦合，此类框架的端到端训练非常不稳定。在跨模式场景中，这个问题会被放大，其中大多数方法严重依赖预训练。在本文中，我们通过相互加权策略重新耦合多模态特征学习的检测和描述的独立约束，其中鲁棒特征的检测概率被迫达到峰值并重复，而在优化过程中强调检测分数高的特征。与之前的工作不同，这些权重与反向传播分离，因此检测到的不明确特征的概率不会被直接抑制，训练会更加稳定。此外，我们提出了超级检测器，一种具有大感受野并配备可学习非极大值抑制层的检测器，以满足严格的检测条件。最后，我们建立了一个包含交叉可见光、红外、近红外和合成孔径雷达图像对的基准，用于评估特征匹配和图像配准任务中特征的性能。大量实验表明，使用名为 ReDFeat 的重新检测和描述训练的特征超越了基准测试中之前的最先进技术，同时该模型可以轻松地从头开始训练。 本文提出ReDFeat方法，通过解耦检测与描述的约束策略，提升多模态特征学习在跨模态图像匹配中的性能。 Yuxin Deng,Jiayi Ma cs.CV,cs.AI

title: Roman: Making Everyday Objects Robotically Manipulable with 3D-Printable Add-on Mechanisms
title_zh: Roman：利用可 3D 打印的附加机制让日常物品变得可机器人操作
abstract: One important vision of robotics is to provide physical assistance by manipulating different everyday objects, e.g., hand tools, kitchen utensils. However, many objects designed for dexterous hand-control are not easily manipulable by a single robotic arm with a generic parallel gripper. Complementary to existing research on developing grippers and control algorithms, we present Roman, a suite of hardware design and software tool support for robotic engineers to create 3D printable mechanisms attached to everyday handheld objects, making them easier to be manipulated by conventional robotic arms. The Roman hardware comes with a versatile magnetic gripper that can snap on/off handheld objects and drive add-on mechanisms to perform tasks. Roman also provides software support to register and author control programs. To validate our approach, we designed and fabricated Roman mechanisms for 14 everyday objects/tasks presented within a design space and conducted expert interviews with robotic engineers indicating that Roman serves as a practical alternative for enabling robotic manipulation of everyday objects.
summary: One important vision of robotics is to provide physical assistance by manipulating different everyday objects, e.g., hand tools, kitchen utensils. However, many objects designed for dexterous hand-control are not easily manipulable by a single robotic arm with a generic parallel gripper. Complementary to existing research on developing grippers and control algorithms, we present Roman, a suite of hardware design and software tool support for robotic engineers to create 3D printable mechanisms attached to everyday handheld objects, making them easier to be manipulated by conventional robotic arms. The Roman hardware comes with a versatile magnetic gripper that can snap on/off handheld objects and drive add-on mechanisms to perform tasks. Roman also provides software support to register and author control programs. To validate our approach, we designed and fabricated Roman mechanisms for 14 everyday objects/tasks presented within a design space and conducted expert interviews with robotic engineers indicating that Roman serves as a practical alternative for enabling robotic manipulation of everyday objects.
abstract_zh: 机器人技术的一个重要愿景是通过操纵不同的日常物体（例如手动工具、厨房用具）来提供物理帮助。然而，许多专为灵巧手控制而设计的物体并不容易通过具有通用平行夹具的单个机械臂来操纵。作为对开发夹具和控制算法的现有研究的补充，我们推出了 Roman，这是一套硬件设计和软件工具支持，为机器人工程师创建附加到日常手持物体上的 3D 打印机构，使它们更容易被传统机械臂操纵。 Roman 硬件配备了多功能磁性夹具，可以卡住/断开手持物体并驱动附加机构来执行任务。 Roman 还提供软件支持来注册和编写控制程序。为了验证我们的方法，我们为设计空间中呈现的 14 种日常物体/任务设计和制造了 Roman 机构，并对机器人工程师进行了专家访谈，表明 Roman 可以作为机器人操纵日常物体的实用替代方案。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为Roman的系统，通过3D打印附加机械装置使日常物品更容易被机械臂操作。
AI.motivation: 现有机械臂难以操作为人类灵巧手设计的日常物品，需开发无需改造物体的实用解决方案。
AI.method: 设计磁性夹爪与软件工具，支持工程师创建3D打印附加装置并编写控制程序以实现物体操作。
AI.result: 为14种日常物品设计了Roman机制，并通过专家访谈验证其作为机器人操作替代方案的实用性。
AI.conclusion: Roman通过可打印附加装置有效解决了机器人操作日常物品的难题，为该领域提供了可行方案。
